findings.evidence: Line 49 is exactly `fixingSubgroup (M ≃ₘ^n⟮I, I⟯ M) (I.boundary M)`; `rg` finds no existing `fixedOnSubgroup`/set-relative wrapper in `code/TauCeti`, while Mathlib exposes the generic `mem_fixingSubgroup_iff` used at line 55.
findings.fix: Introduce `Diffeomorph.fixedOnSubgroup (I) (n) (s : Set M) : Subgroup (M ≃ₘ^n⟮I, I⟯ M) := fixingSubgroup _ s`, prove `mem_fixedOnSubgroup_iff`, and define `boundaryFixingSubgroup I n := fixedOnSubgroup I n (I.boundary M)`.
findings.issue: `boundaryFixingSubgroup` hard-codes `I.boundary M`, but the same construction is the relative subgroup fixing any `s : Set M`; the boundary case is only a specialization.

findings.evidence: Lines 60-64 target `fixingSubgroup (M ≃ₜ M) (I.boundary M)` and prove it by `mem_fixingSubgroup_iff` plus `toHomeomorphHom_apply`; no line uses `ModelWithCorners.boundary` beyond the chosen set.
findings.fix: State the theorem for arbitrary `s : Set M`, e.g. `toHomeomorphHom_mem_fixedOnSubgroup {s} {f} (hf : f ∈ fixedOnSubgroup I n s) : toHomeomorphHom f ∈ fixingSubgroup (M ≃ₜ M) s`, then derive the boundary corollary if desired.
findings.issue: `toHomeomorphHom_mem_boundaryFixingSubgroup` is also a boundary-only theorem, but its proof uses only pointwise fixing of a subset, not any boundary fact.

summary: The new API is boundary-specialized even though the construction and forgetful compatibility are naturally set-relative. Add the general fixed-on-subset subgroup and derive the boundary names from it.
